subject: Mathematics
title: Why Primes Matter
description: Learn Why Primes Matter in one minute.

hook.text: Every time you shop online, prime numbers protect your credit card data.

buildup.text: Multiplying two large primes is easy. Reversing that multiplication is nearly impossible.

discovery.text: RSA encryption relies on this asymmetry. Banks use primes to lock your data.

twist.text: If someone found a fast way to factor large numbers, internet security would collapse.

climax.text: Quantum computers may eventually crack RSA, pushing cryptographers to find new defenses.

punchline.text: Your password is guarded by numbers that refuse to divide.

quiz.question: Why are primes important to online security?
options: They make encryption hard to reverse
